name: The Merchants & Planters Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank serving Minnesota and Wisconsin with over 150 years of history, offering personal banking, mortgages, business loans, and credit products.
description_long:
Merchants Bank is a regional community bank with more than 150 years of operating history, primarily serving Minnesota and Wisconsin markets. The bank maintains 21 branch locations across these two states, with significant presence in the Winona, Rochester, and Northfield areas of Minnesota, plus locations in Eau Claire and Onalaska, Wisconsin. The institution is FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing standard deposit insurance protections.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including personal checking and savings accounts, mortgage lending (purchase, refinance, and construction), personal and small business loans, credit cards, commercial banking services, and trust/investment accounts. Their online and mobile banking platforms serve personal, small business, and commercial customers with 24-hour telephone banking available. They provide specialized services including dealer floor plans, credit card programs, and commercial treasury management.

Merchants Bank distinguishes itself through its community-focused approach and longevity in regional markets. The website emphasizes business partnerships and local impact through case studies like the Thern company story. The bank provides multiple customer service channels including phone support (with varying hours by service line), online chat (Monday-Friday 8am-6pm, Saturday 8am-noon), and in-person branch visits. Their routing number is 091900193.

As a regional community bank, Merchants Bank serves traditional banking needs but operates within a limited geographic footprint. While they offer competitive products and local decision-making advantages typical of community banks, customers should verify specific product availability and rates at their nearest branch, as offerings may vary. The bank's strength lies in personal relationships and understanding local business needs rather than cutting-edge fintech or best-in-class rates across all products.
services: Personal loans and lines of credit
Small business loans and lines of credit
Online and mobile banking (personal and business)
Commercial treasury management and cash management
Telephone banking (24/7 account access and transfers)
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full U.S. Government backing
Over 150 years of established banking history and local market presence
21 branch locations across Minnesota and Wisconsin for in-person service
Multiple contact channels including phone, chat (extended hours including Saturday mornings), and branches
Comprehensive product suite including mortgages, personal loans, business loans, and credit cards
24-hour telephone banking access for account inquiries and transfers
Commercial banking services including treasury management and dealer floor plans for business customers
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to Minnesota and Wisconsin only
No mention of competitive rates or rate guarantees compared to national banks
Limited information about specific loan products, terms, APRs, or fees on public website
Smaller institution may have less technological innovation than larger national banks
Website lacks details on minimum account balances, overdraft fees, or other standard banking costs
best_for: Minnesota and Wisconsin residents seeking community banking with local branch access
Small to mid-size business owners needing commercial lending and relationship banking
Homebuyers in their service areas looking for mortgage products and local loan decisions
Customers valuing personal banker relationships over digital-first banking experiences
diagnosis: Merchants Bank is best for Minnesota and Wisconsin residents and small-to-mid-size business owners who value community banking relationships, local decision-making, and in-person service. The primary caveat is geographic limitation to two states—this institution cannot serve customers outside Minnesota and Wisconsin, making it unsuitable for those needing national or multi-state banking presence.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
